journal_entry.description: Board declared cash dividend of $500,000, payable in 30 days
journal_entry.rationale: Upon declaration, a dividend creates a legal liability to shareholders. The debit to Dividends Declared (or Retained Earnings) reduces equity, and the credit to Dividends Payable establishes a current liability. The dividend will be paid in 30 days, at which time Dividends Payable will be debited and Cash credited. This follows IFRS recognition principle that a liability is recognized when the board formally declares the dividend.

ambiguities.resolution: 'declared' is a conventional term indicating the dividend payable has been established but not yet paid — creates a liability (Dividends Payable) until the payment date in 30 days

output.reason: When a dividend is declared but not yet paid, the company records a liability (Dividends Payable). The credit side records the liability increase. The debit side would record the dividend declaration (dividend increase), but we only classify credits here.

credit_classifier.reason: When a dividend is declared but not yet paid, the company records a liability (Dividends Payable). The credit side records the liability increase. The debit side would record the dividend declaration (dividend increase), but we only classify credits here.

output.reason: Declaration of cash dividend creates a debit to Dividends (or Retained Earnings), which represents a dividend increase. The $500,000 dividend declared is 1 debit line increasing dividends. The credit side (not classified here) would be to Dividends Payable liability.

debit_classifier.reason: Declaration of cash dividend creates a debit to Dividends (or Retained Earnings), which represents a dividend increase. The $500,000 dividend declared is 1 debit line increasing dividends. The credit side (not classified here) would be to Dividends Payable liability.
